title: olearia canescens subsp. canescens
intro: Olearia canescens subsp. canescens, also known as the woolly tree daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in New Zealand and Australia, for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Olearia canescens subsp. canescens is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
